body_txt: War Is Hell for many, and they would rather it didn't happen, for reasons anywhere from war being unnecessary, to them wishing it wasn't necessary. In fiction, however, some villains not only like war , they frequently like to start wars for their own ends. Their motives may vary. They may set out to profit from the situation , or trick their enemies into destroying each other , or even try to Take Over the World in the aftermath. They may be terrorists, warlords , or just Card Carrying Villains . The method is often some form of False Flag Operation ; the villains pretend to be members of one country, and attack another. Nuclear weapons are commonly used in the set-up. At times, both sides in a conflict use a flimsy Pretext for War just so they can get to the "good" parts. Heroes typically struggle to Prevent the War , or end it as bloodlessly and quickly as possible. It's sometimes implied in narrative examples of this trope that corporations profit from this war, e.g arms manufacturers. Then again, the opposite can also be true, (relative) peace tends to make arms markets flat — a long-standing threat of war (real or artificial ) can be damned profitable for arms manufacturers. There's a reason this trope doesn't have a Real Life section. Compare Corporate Warfare , where corporations cut out the middleman and do the fighting themselves, and Blood Knight , where the fun comes from the fighting itself. See also Avoiding the Great War , when this coincides with WWI.
